seo.title: Building Research Agents with CrewAI + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build research agents with CrewAI + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: CrewAI enables building collaborative Research Agents that perform web searches, analyze data, and synthesize insights across multiple specialized roles. Guardrails are essential to prevent delegation loops, hallucinated outputs, task drift, and inconsistent quality in multi-agent research workflows. Vex integration ensures reliable outputs through automated verification of sources, claims, recency, and bias.

architectureOverview.heading: Research Agents Architecture with CrewAI
architectureOverview.description:
Research Agents in CrewAI typically involve a Researcher agent that conducts web searches and gathers sources, an Analyst agent that extracts claims and verifies facts, and a Synthesizer agent that compiles summaries with citations. Tasks flow sequentially with delegation: Researcher → Analyst → Synthesizer, using tools like Serper for search and custom verification functions. This modular design handles complex information synthesis but risks error propagation without verification.

Each agent has defined roles, goals, and backstories, with tasks including human-readable outputs and JSON structuring for downstream processing. The Crew orchestrates execution, kicking off with the Researcher and delegating based on task outputs. Vex callbacks intercept task outputs for verification before delegation.

risks.name: Agent delegation loops
risks.description: Agents endlessly pass incomplete research tasks between roles without resolution
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies task completion before delegation with progress tracking

risks.name: Hallucinated tool outputs
risks.description: Search tools return fabricated URLs or content passed between agents
risks.mitigation: Vex validates source accessibility and content authenticity

risks.name: Task drift
risks.description: Agents reinterpret research objectives, focusing on irrelevant subtopics
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces objective alignment in task outputs

risks.name: Inconsistent output quality
risks.description: Different agents produce varying citation formats and verification rigor
risks.mitigation: Vex standardizes output schema and verification depth

implementation.title: Basic Research Crew Setup
implementation.description: Define agents and tasks for the research workflow with Serper search tool
implementation.code:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
from crewai_tools import SerperDevTool
import os

os.environ['SERPER_API_KEY'] = 'your-key'

researcher = Agent(
    role='Lead Researcher',
    goal='Find authoritative sources on {topic}',
    backstory='Expert in web research with 20+ years experience',
    tools=[SerperDevTool()],  
    verbose=True
)

analyst = Agent(
    role='Fact Analyst',
    goal='Verify claims and extract key insights',
    backstory='PhD researcher specializing in information validation',
    verbose=True
)

task1 = Task(
    description='Research latest developments on {topic}',
    agent=researcher
)

task2 = Task(
    description='Analyze sources from researcher for accuracy',
    agent=analyst
)

crew = Crew(agents=[researcher, analyst], tasks=[task1, task2])

implementation.title: Research Task with Structured Output
implementation.description: Enforce JSON output format for reliable agent-to-agent communication

implementation.title: Custom Verification Tool
implementation.description: Agent tool for basic source validation before synthesis

implementation.title: Crew Execution with Output Parsing
implementation.description: Run crew and parse structured research results
implementation.code:
research_result = crew.kickoff(inputs={'topic': 'AI agent reliability 2024'})

# Parse structured output
sources = research_result.json()['sources']
insights = research_result.json()['key_claims']

print(f"Found {len(sources)} sources")
print("Key insights:", insights)

# Export to markdown
with open('research_report.md', 'w') as f:
    f.write(f"# Research Report: AI Agent Reliability\n\n")
    for source in sources:
        f.write(f"- [{source['title']}]({source['url']})\n")

vexIntegration.title: Vex Task Callback for Source Verification
vexIntegration.description: Intercept researcher outputs to verify source accessibility and authority
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import VexClient
from crewai import Task

vex = VexClient(api_key='your-vex-key')

def vex_source_check(task_output: str, task: Task):
    """Verify sources before analyst receives them"""
    try:
        data = task.json_output
        sources = data.get('sources', [])
        
        for source in sources[:5]:  # Check top 5
            result = vex.verify({
                'type': 'source_accessibility',
                'url': source['url'],
                'domain_authority_check': True,
                'recency_days': 90
            })
            
            if not result['valid']:
                print(f"VEX BLOCKED: {source['url']} - {result['reason']}")
                return False
        return True
    except:
        return False

research_task.callbacks.append(vex_source_check)

vexIntegration.title: Vex Claim Accuracy Check
vexIntegration.description: Validate factual claims extracted by analyst agent

vexIntegration.title: Vex Bias + Recency Guardrail
vexIntegration.description: Final synthesis check for bias detection and content freshness
vexIntegration.code:
def vex_final_synthesis_guard(task_output: str, task: Task):
    """Ensure final report passes bias and recency checks"""
    data = task.json_output
    
    # Recency check
    recency_result = vex.verify({
        'type': 'content_recency',
        'sources': data['sources'],
        'max_age_days': 180,
        'topic': 'AI agent reliability'
    })
    
    if not recency_result['fresh']:
        print("VEX BLOCKED: Content too outdated")
        return False
    
    # Bias detection
    bias_result = vex.verify({
        'type': 'bias_detection',
        'content': data['summary'],
        'sources': data['sources'],
        'bias_threshold': 'medium'
    })
    
    if bias_result['bias_score'] > 0.3:
        print(f"VEX FLAGGED: High bias detected ({bias_result['bias_score']})")
        return False
    
    print("✅ VEX: All checks passed")
    return True

synthesis_task.callbacks.append(vex_final_synthesis_guard)

# Run secure crew
final_report = crew.kickoff(inputs={'topic': 'AI agent reliability 2024'})
